Article Title: Neymar likely to miss Brazil World Cup 2026 opener due to serious calf injury confirmed in medical scan- reports
Article Text:
Santos' Neymar grimaces in pain after he was fouled during a Brazilian Championship soccer match against Coritiba in Sao Paulo, Sunday, May 17, 2026. (AP Photo/Andre Penner)

MRI confirms grade-two calf injury for Neymar

Brazil establishes deadline before final squad decision

Neymar injury dominates Ancelotti’s early Brazil camp

Neymar publicly downplays concerns despite growing uncertainty

Santos' Neymar grimaces in pain after he was fouled during a Brazilian Championship soccer match against Coritiba in Sao Paulo, Sunday, May 17, 2026. (AP Photo/Andre Penner)

Brazil hoping Neymar can return for another World Cup run

Brazil’s preparations for the 2026 FIFA World Cup have been overshadowed by fresh concerns surrounding Neymar after reports confirmed the Santos forward is dealing with a more serious calf injury than initially expected.The 34-year-old arrived at Brazil’s training camp under intense medical scrutiny after suffering another muscular issue in his right calf, with the situation quickly becoming the dominant storyline around Carlo Ancelotti’s squad before the tournament begins in the United States, Mexico and Canada.According to multiple reports in Brazil, Neymar is now expected to miss Brazil’s two pre-World Cup friendlies against Panama and Egypt and could also be unavailable for the Seleção’s opening Group J match against Morocco on June 13 in New Jersey.The Brazilian Football Confederation (CBF) confirmed Neymar did not participate in closed training sessions at Granja Comary and instead underwent further imaging examinations at a private clinic in Teresópolis.|Brazil team doctor Rodrigo Lasmar later revealed that Neymar’s examinations identified a more significant injury than previously believed.“Yesterday Neymar underwent all the medical tests, which concluded with an MRI scan that revealed a grade two calf injury, not just edema.He is expected to recover in two or three weeks,” Lasmar said in a statement released in Teresópolis.According to Marca , the diagnosis differs considerably from the initial information Santos had provided to the CBF before Neymar reported to the national team camp.Santos doctor Rodrigo Zogaib had reportedly informed Brazil’s coaching staff that Neymar was only dealing with edema and would be fully fit to begin training with the squad on May 27, which ultimately did not happen.Further reports now suggest the recovery timeline could stretch closer to three or even four weeks depending on how the calf responds to treatment, creating growing uncertainty around Neymar’s availability for the early stages of the tournament.According to information published by Torcedores, Brazil’s coaching staff has already established a definitive internal deadline to determine whether Neymar will remain part of the World Cup squad.The key date is expected to be Brazil’s friendly against Egypt on June 6 in the United States, which will reportedly serve as the final major assessment of Neymar’s condition before a definitive decision is made.FIFA regulations allow injury replacements up to 24 hours before a nation’s opening World Cup match, meaning Brazil still has flexibility if Neymar fails to recover in time.If the more optimistic recovery projection of approximately two weeks proves accurate, Neymar could potentially return around June 11, only two days before Brazil’s opening match against Morocco.Brazil’s second group-stage fixture is scheduled against Haiti on June 19.Neymar’s condition has already created significant tension around Brazil’s opening camp sessions.According to reports from Brazil, the Brazilian federation even temporarily closed sections of the medical facility being used for Neymar’s examinations to guarantee complete privacy during the testing process.The situation has also placed additional attention on Carlo Ancelotti’s decision to include Neymar in the World Cup squad despite previously insisting he would only call up players who were fully fit.The former Real Madrid, Chelsea, Paris Saint-Germain and AC Milan manager had omitted Neymar from earlier Brazil squads while repeatedly stressing that only players at “100 per cent physical capacity” would be selected.However, Ancelotti still chose to include the Santos forward in his 26-man World Cup squad, with reports describing emotional scenes inside Neymar’s household when his name was officially announced.According to reports, Ancelotti has since held direct conversations with Neymar regarding how the recovery process will be managed throughout the tournament.Neymar has reportedly agreed to remain with the squad even if he initially assumes a reduced role while continuing rehabilitation work alongside Brazil’s medical staff, Santos doctors and his personal specialists.Despite the growing concern surrounding his condition, Neymar has continued attempting to publicly project confidence.After watching Santos defeat Deportivo Cuenca 3-0 in a Copa Sudamericana match at Vila Belmiro earlier this week, Neymar was asked by reporters about the condition of his calf.“It’s right here, in one piece,” Neymar replied.When questioned whether the injury could jeopardise his World Cup participation, the forward responded: “What problem?”Behind the scenes, however, Brazil’s medical department is reportedly taking a far more cautious approach.Sources told ESPN that the swelling in Neymar’s right calf “is not minor” and that the recovery period may ultimately prove longer than Santos initially anticipated.Neymar has not represented Brazil since suffering an ACL injury during international duty in October 2023.The Santos forward remains Brazil’s all-time leading scorer with 79 international goals and is preparing for what would be his fourth World Cup appearance.His selection itself had already generated major discussion because of his lengthy absence from the national team and ongoing injury concerns.The call-up also ensured Neymar’s return to the World Cup stage after Brazil’s disappointing recent campaigns.Brazil finished fourth during the 2014 World Cup on home soil before suffering quarter-final eliminations in both 2018 and 2022.The Seleção now begin another attempt to secure a record-extending sixth World Cup title, although much of the attention surrounding the squad currently remains fixed on Neymar’s recovery timeline and whether Brazil’s biggest star will be fully fit when the tournament begins.
Article Summary: Santos forward Neymar is dealing with a more serious calf injury than initially expected. The 34-year-old is now expected to miss Brazil’s two pre-World Cup friendlies against Panama and Egypt. Neymar could also be unavailable for the Seleção's opening Group J match against Morocco on June 13 in New Jersey. The Brazilian Football Confederation confirmed Neymar did not participate in closed training sessions.

Article Title: "I’m revealing everything": Dianna Russini's affair with Mike Vrabel may just be the tip of the iceberg, fired journalist Crissy Froyd now warns
Article Text:
Dianna Russini; Crissy Froyd. (Getty Images)

What did Crissy Froyd reveal about the NFL media S*x scandal?



Crissy Froyd and Dianna Russini & Mike Vrabel (Getty Images)

What did Froyd reveal about her firing from USA Today?



Crissy Froyd isn't staying quiet. Weeks after being fired from USA Today Sports for publicly criticizing Dianna Russini, the veteran NFL reporter has resurfaced at the Daily Mail with a piece that pulls no punches. Her headline alone signals what's coming: "I was fired for exposing the NFL's dirtiest sex secret. Now, I'm revealing everything… and naming names." She has details, a timeline, and a clear message for anyone who thought this story was going away.Froyd’s latest article was framed around disappointment with how reporters handled Vrabel’s recent media availability. She said she expected at least one direct question about the controversy surrounding his alleged relationship with Russini. Instead, she came away convinced that many journalists had no interest in pursuing the matter.“When the NFL’s Mike Vrabel, head coach of the New England Patriots, stepped up to the microphones to face reporters on Wednesday, I held my breath,” she wrote.Her frustration grew when the topic never surfaced.“It appears that no one is particularly interested in getting to the bottom of this story,” Froyd claimed.“Back in the New England Patriot news conference room the one question was never asked.Instead, the reporters danced around the topic.”From there, Froyd broadened her criticism beyond a single incident. She argued that rumors involving reporters and NFL employees have long circulated throughout the industry and suggested that media organizations have largely avoided confronting the issue. According to Froyd, she first heard claims about Vrabel and Russini around 2020 and later encountered similar stories involving other reporters and league personnel.“Around 2020, I was told by a well-known national sports reporter in explicit terms that Vrabel, then coach of the Tennessee Titans, and Russini were carrying on a secret relationship,” she wrote.She added that multiple female reporters had privately told her about relationships with NFL staff members, including, in one instance, a prominent head coach.Near the end of her column, Froyd connected the controversy directly to her own departure from USA Today. She said her criticism of Russini ultimately led to the loss of her job and argued that the fallout has affected the credibility of reporters who maintain professional boundaries.“The Vrabel-Russini scandal also casts suspicion over the journalists and NFL teams that do things that right way, of which there are many. That’s unfair and demoralizing. That’s why I tweeted about Russini’s resignation from The Athletic, resulting in my termination from USA Today. Even more disheartening, it appears that no one is particularly interested in getting to the bottom of this story.”Froyd also claimed she personally experienced inappropriate advances during her reporting career. She described situations in which professional interactions allegedly shifted into personal invitations and suggested that some coaches used subtle tactics to test boundaries with reporters covering their teams. Her comments have added another layer to a debate that continues to generate discussion across NFL media circles, even as key figures involved have offered little new public response.
Article Summary: Crissy Froyd was fired from USA Today Sports for publicly criticizing Dianna Russini. She resurfaced at the Daily Mail with a piece that pulls no punches. Her headline alone signals what's coming: "I was fired for exposing the NFL's dirtiest sex secret"

Article Title: "You’re making Millions of Dollars": How Donald Trump's defense of Jaxson Dart turned a Giants QB into a hot-selling phenomenon
Article Text:
Donald Trump Defends Jaxson Dart After Rally Controversy Overshadows Giants OTA (Getty Images)

What did Donald Trump say in defense of Jaxson Dart?



Can Jaxson Dart and the Giants move past the offseason distractions?



New York Giants quarterback Jaxson Dart introduced President Donald Trump at a May 22 rally in New York for Representative Mike Lawler, setting off a chain of events that has consumed the Giants' entire offseason practice period. Teammate Abdul Carter posted a now-deleted reaction on X, the team held a private meeting, and Dart addressed reporters Friday. Then Trump entered the conversation himself, posting a full defense of the 23-year-old on Truth Social.Trump weighed in directly on the situation through Truth Social, offering strong support for the Giants quarterback while criticizing those who have challenged him since the rally.“Thank you Jaxson! It was great being with you,” Trump said. “I know you’re taking some heat from the Radical Left Lunatics who are jealous of you, me, and everyone who surrounds us but, l also know that your Jersey also went to Number One, and you’re making Millions of Dollars so, YOU ARE A WINNER – THEY ARE ALL LOSERS. I’ll see you in the White House!”The president's comments arrived just one day after Dart publicly addressed the criticism for the first time.Rather than engage in political debate, the 23-year-old stressed that his decision was rooted in respect for the office of the presidency.“Obviously this was a unique opportunity, being asked and given the opportunity to introduce the President of the United States…The president position has always been a position I’ve well respected regardless of political affiliation, regardless of political party and my intentions were just that.”Dart also pointed to his family's history of military and public service while explaining his decision. Throughout his remarks, he repeatedly emphasized respect, unity, and maintaining strong relationships inside the Giants locker room despite differing political views.For New York, that question matters more than any social media debate.The Giants are attempting to rebound from a disappointing 4-13 season under new head coach John Harbaugh, who was brought in to establish a stronger culture and restore competitiveness. Instead, the franchise has spent much of its OTA period dealing with headlines unrelated to football.Dart remains one of the team's biggest reasons for optimism. As a rookie, he threw for 2,272 yards, 15 touchdowns, and only five interceptions while adding 487 rushing yards and nine scores on the ground. His 76.3 QB Impact Metric from PFSN ranked him 18th among NFL quarterbacks, reflecting the promise he showed in his first season.Still, production is ultimately what will determine how long this story lingers. Dart acknowledged that politics can be divisive but stressed that the Giants have maintained open dialogue behind closed doors.“I also understand in this world, politics can be a sensitive matter, a sensitive topic,” Dart said before adding that he values every teammate regardless of political or religious beliefs. He described the team's bond as a genuine brotherhood built on honest conversations and mutual respect.A private team meeting this week underscored how seriously the organization has treated the issue. Yet inside the building, the focus remains on football. If Dart delivers the second-year leap many expect, attention will likely shift back to his development as a quarterback. If the Giants struggle, however, this offseason controversy may continue to follow both him and the franchise.
Article Summary: New York Giants quarterback Jaxson Dart introduced President Donald Trump at a May 22 rally. Teammate Abdul Carter posted a now-deleted reaction on X, the team held a private meeting, and Dart addressed reporters Friday. Then Trump entered the conversation himself, posting a full defense of the 23-year-old on Truth Social.

Article Title: NFL Trade Rumors: Kansas City Chiefs linked to $10M Chicago Bears TE and $5M New York Giants pass rusher in proposed deals
Article Text:
The Kansas City Chiefs have been linked to two potential trade targets in ESPN's offseason simulation. One proposal would bring Chicago Bears tight end Cole Kmet to Kansas City for a 2027 third-round pick, while another would send New York Giants edge rusher Kayvon Thibodeaux to the Chiefs for a 2027 fourth-rounder. The rumors address Kansas City's needs at tight end depth behind Travis Kelce and pass-rush help entering the 2026 season.





Category Details Player Cole Kmet / Kayvon Thibodeaux Current Team Chicago Bears / New York Giants Rumored Team Kansas City Chiefs Contract Status Under contract Salary Cap Hit Existing veteran contracts Trade Likelihood Moderate to low Latest Insider Update ESPN simulated both trade scenarios Potential Return 2027 third-round pick and fifth-round swap for Kmet; 2027 fourth-round pick for Thibodeaux

Which teams are interested in Cole Kmet and Kayvon Thibodeaux?

What insiders are saying about the trade rumors

Contract details and salary cap implications

How the trade could impact both teams

The Kansas City Chiefs have once again found themselves at the center of offseason trade speculation, with ESPN proposing two potential moves that could address key roster needs before the 2026 season. One scenario would send a 2027 third-round pick to the Chicago Bears for tight end Cole Kmet and a 2027 fifth-round pick.Another would see Kansas City acquire New York Giants pass rusher Kayvon Thibodeaux for a 2027 fourth-round selection. While neither deal is currently being negotiated, the proposals have sparked discussion about whether general manager Brett Veach should pursue outside help before training camp.The Chiefs are the only team directly connected to either player in the ESPN simulation, but both names fit areas where Kansas City could use reinforcement.Kmet would address concerns behind Travis Kelce . Although Kelce remains one of the league's premier offensive weapons, the depth chart behind him is far less proven. Noah Gray has shown flashes, while Jared Wiley remains an unknown at the NFL level. Adding Kmet would give Kansas City another reliable blocker and pass catcher, particularly if Andy Reid continues emphasizing a more balanced offensive approach.Thibodeaux fills a different need. The Chiefs have invested heavily in young edge rushers over recent drafts, yet questions remain about consistent pass-rush production opposite their established veterans. A player with Thibodeaux's pedigree could offer upside in a rotational role while providing another pass-rushing option for defensive coordinator Steve Spagnuolo.ESPN's Ben Solak proposed both deals as part of a league-wide trade market exercise.Regarding Kmet, Solak argued that Kansas City has a significant need at tight end behind Kelce and could benefit from adding a more traditional in-line blocker. He also pointed to Kmet's familiarity with Chiefs offensive coordinator Eric Bieniemy.On Thibodeaux, Solak suggested Kansas City could take another swing on a former first-round talent. However, Arrowhead Pride's Ron Kopp Jr. questioned whether the Giants could realistically command a Day 2 pick for a player who has largely been a rotational contributor outside of his standout 11.5-sack campaign.Both players remain under team control, which adds value from Kansas City's perspective. Neither player is known to have a no-trade clause that would complicate a potential move.Kmet's contract would provide cost certainty at a position where the Chiefs may soon need to prepare for life after Kelce. Thibodeaux's deal also offers flexibility, though Kansas City would need to determine whether his production justifies the financial commitment and draft capital required.The Chiefs currently have enough roster flexibility to absorb either contract, but every move would need to fit within Veach's long-term cap planning.A Kmet acquisition would likely have a more immediate impact. It would allow Kansas City to use multiple tight-end formations more frequently while reducing the workload on Kelce during the regular season.For Chicago, adding another third-round selection could create additional draft flexibility as the franchise continues building around its young core.A Thibodeaux trade would be more about upside. Kansas City would be betting on Spagnuolo's ability to unlock the former fifth-overall pick's potential, while the Giants would gain future draft assets and additional roster flexibility.At this stage, both proposals remain hypothetical. Still, they highlight the areas where the Chiefs could look for reinforcements as another Super Bowl chase begins to take shape.
Article Summary: The Kansas City Chiefs have been linked to two potential trade targets in ESPN's offseason simulation. One proposal would bring Chicago Bears tight end Cole Kmet to Kansas City for a 2027 third-round pick. Another would send New York Giants edge rusher Kayvon Thibodeaux to the Chiefs for a2027 fourth-rounder. The rumors address Kansas City's needs at tight end depth behind Travis Kelce and pass-rush help.

Article Title: NFL Trade Rumors: $10 Million Pittsburgh Steelers linebacker addresses trade speculation after turbulent offseason
Article Text:
Pittsburgh Steelers linebacker Patrick Queen dismissed offseason trade rumors, calling much of the speculation a “charade” fueled by social media. Entering the final year of his contract, Queen said there have been no major extension talks and he remains focused on football. The two-time Pro Bowler stressed that he is unbothered by his uncertain future, adding that whether the Steelers extend him or not, “it’s a business.”





Category Details Player Patrick Queen Current Team Pittsburgh Steelers Rumored Team Multiple teams discussed in speculation Contract Status Final year of 3-year contract Salary Cap Hit Significant veteran cap figure Trade Likelihood Low at present Latest Insider Update No active trade movement reported Potential Return Draft capital and defensive assets

Which teams are interested in Patrick Queen?

What insiders are saying about the trade rumors

Contract details and salary cap implications

How the situation impacts the Steelers

Patrick Queen is not spending much time worrying about trade rumors. After weeks of speculation linking the Pittsburgh Steelers linebacker to a possible move elsewhere, the two-time Pro Bowler made it clear that his focus remains on football, not offseason chatter.Questions about Queen's future surfaced earlier this offseason as he entered the final year of his contract. With no extension in place and a sizable cap number attached to his deal, some analysts floated the possibility of a trade or even a roster move. Despite the noise, Queen remains firmly in Pittsburgh as the Steelers continue preparations for the 2026 season.No specific franchise has emerged as a serious trade destination for Queen, but his profile naturally attracts attention around the league. At just 26 years old, he combines durability, production, and experience at a premium defensive position.Teams seeking help at linebacker would undoubtedly have interest if Pittsburgh ever made him available. Queen has started 101 consecutive games since entering the NFL and has recorded five 100-tackle seasons in six years. That type of consistency is difficult to find.From a roster-building perspective, contenders looking to strengthen the middle of their defense would view Queen as an immediate starter.However, there is little evidence that the Steelers are actively shopping him.Speaking during organized team activities, Queen downplayed the entire situation."I saw the whole charade that went on all this offseason, but I mean it's talks. Obviously, no movement either way," he said.The linebacker acknowledged that some reports contained elements of truth but believes social media amplified the speculation beyond reality."Some of it was true, but some of it was extended truth, just people rambling and stuff," Queen said. "That's what you can have with social media nowadays. You know, everybody wants the first say whatever happens -- and wants to hope to be right. [There were] very [few] facts out there."Queen is entering the final season of his current deal, which explains why rumors surfaced in the first place. He confirmed there have been no major developments regarding a contract extension."There has been nothing crazy" in negotiations, according to Queen.Still, he does not appear concerned about his long-term future."I've got a good amount of money coming in this year," Queen said. "So at the end of the day, I couldn't care less. If they do want to extend, cool. If not, cool. At the end of the day, it's a business."For Pittsburgh, keeping Queen is the preferred outcome. He remains one of the defense's most reliable players and finished near the top of the team tackle rankings again last season.With Mike McCarthy beginning his first year as head coach and Aaron Rodgers expected to play his final NFL season, stability on defense could be critical. Unless negotiations change dramatically, Queen's comments suggest both sides are comfortable moving forward together, leaving trade speculation with little substance heading into the season.
Article Summary: Pittsburgh Steelers linebacker Patrick Queen dismissed offseason trade rumors. Queen said there have been no major extension talks and he remains focused on football. The two-time Pro Bowler stressed that he is unbothered by his uncertain future. No specific franchise has emerged as a serious trade destination for Queen.

Article Title: "Bad things are gonna happen": NFL legend warns Travis Kelce and Patrick Mahomes over Chiefs' handling of Rashee Rice
Article Text:
Former NFL star Jarvis Landry challenged Chiefs leaders Travis Kelce and Patrick Mahomes to mentor Rashee Rice as the wide receiver deals with legal troubles and an uncertain future. Landry stressed the importance of surrounding Rice with positive influences and guidance. Jarvis Landry calls on Travis Kelce and Patrick Mahomes to guide Rashee Rice

Rashee Rice's Chiefs future remains uncertain despite Andy Reid 's support

The Kansas City Chiefs are facing tough questions again about one of their star players. This time, it’s wide receiver Rashee Rice, whose legal troubles have sparked new discussion about accountability, leadership and second chances within one of the NFL’s most successful franchises.As Rice deals with the consequences of his off-field issues, a former Super Bowl champion believes the conversation should extend beyond the player himself. Instead, he wants some of the biggest names in Kansas City to play a larger role in helping the young receiver find a better path forward.Former New Orleans Saints receiver Jarvis Landry did not hold back when discussing Rice's situation. Speaking on the 4th And South Podcast, Landry challenged the Chiefs' veteran leaders to become more actively involved in the receiver's development away from football."I challenge Travis Kelce, I challenge Patrick Mahomes, I challenge any leader that’s in the Kansas City organization to put their arms around this young man, give him guidance, put him in a better room and better position," Landry said.He continued by stressing the importance of a positive support system."Show him how to move in the offseason and how to establish a circle that can lead to generational wealth because they say you are the sum of who you hang with.If you hang around bad s—, bad s— gonna happen to you. If you’re hanging with bad people, bad things are gonna happen to you."Landry's comments arrive at a time when Rice's future remains uncertain. While many fans have focused on punishment and roster implications, Landry's perspective centers on mentorship and personal growth. His message suggests that leadership inside a locker room should extend beyond game days and team meetings.The Chiefs have publicly avoided making any dramatic decisions regarding Rice. Head coach Andy Reid recently indicated that the organization intends to continue supporting the receiver while expecting him to learn from the experience."As far as Rashee goes, we’re aware of the situation. The league is aware of it. We’ve talked to the league. We get it," Reid said.The veteran coach also emphasized patience."It’s not an easy thing he’s going through. Life lessons are important, but we’re all given chances to learn. He’s in that position now."Still, questions remain. Rice is recovering from injury, dealing with legal consequences, and entering a critical stage of his NFL career. Missing valuable development time could make his return even more challenging.For now, it looks like the Chiefs are riding with their young receiver. Whether that approach pays off ultimately may depend not just on Rice’s talent, but also on the guidance he is given by the leaders around him.
Article Summary: Former NFL star Jarvis Landry challenged Chiefs leaders Travis Kelce and Patrick Mahomes to mentor Rashee Rice. Landry stressed the importance of surrounding Rice with positive influences and guidance. The situation has intensified scrutiny on the Chiefs' leadership and their handling of off-field issues.

Article Title: "Cheating through his phone": Cardi B and Stefon Diggs rocked by explosive rumor that has social media talking
Article Text:
A viral social media post recently claimed Cardi B confronted Stefon Diggs over alleged cheating, sparking widespread discussion online. However, there is no verified evidence supporting the accusation, and neither Cardi B nor Diggs has publicly addressed the rumor. Despite the lack of confirmation, the story spread rapidly across social media, highlighting how quickly unverified celebrity claims can gain traction. For now, the allegations remain speculation rather than established fact.

Cardi B and Stefon Diggs rumor takes off even without evidence

Cardi B and Stefon Diggs are still being talked about on social media

Social media users are talking about the latest online exchange between Cardi B and NFL star Stefon Diggs . The controversy was ignited by a viral post that claimed the duo had an intense argument over the alleged messages found on a phone.But there is no validated proof to substantiate the claim at the moment. There has been no word from Cardi B or Diggs on the rumor and there are no credible reports confirming the alleged incident ever happened. The story is, therefore, nothing more than speculation online.Social media is quick, especially when it involves celebrities. A single post can go viral across platforms within minutes, gathering thousands of reactions before the most basic fact is checked. That seems to be the case with the latest rumor surrounding the rapper and New England Patriots wide receiver.The post that kicked off the convo said: "Allegedly sources are saying late last night Rapper Cardi B caught her boyfriend and her baby daddy Steffon diggs cheating through his phone. It is being said that the two were at their home when suddenly a loud shout was heard when people arrived they saw Cardi having a heated argument with him then she claimed that she saw some texts through his phone with another lady.It was said that Cardi drove off in her car after the whole drama. ”The message got attention quickly but had no proof to back it up, no named sources who could be identified and no photos, videos, police reports or statements to corroborate the claims. But the rumor was going wild and users were busy talking about whether it had any truth or not.It points to a growing problem of celebrity culture. Viral posts often take off long before the facts are known, leaving public figures at the center of stories they may have had no role in creating.Cardi B and Diggs are no strangers to public scrutiny. Their private lives are always in the news, in fan speculation and online theories. But not all trending topics are real.The thing fans and readers need to know most is that the cheating allegation being thrown around online is unsubstantiated. The claim is to be treated as an unconfirmed rumor, not as a known fact, until one of the parties comments or credible reporting surfaces.In an era where speculation can become headlines within hours, thanks to social media, the line between fact and fiction has never been more important.
Article Summary: A viral social media post recently claimed Cardi B confronted Stefon Diggs over alleged cheating, sparking widespread discussion online. There is no verified evidence supporting the accusation, and neither cardi B nor Diggs has publicly addressed the rumor. Despite the lack of confirmation, the story spread rapidly across social media, highlighting how quickly unverified celebrity claims can gain traction.

While the team has remained distant from trade rumors, the Kansas City Chiefs and the New Orleans Saints have been linked to the star player.Over the last few weeks, as per NFL insiders, many rival teams have been reportedly eyeing Kayvon Thibodeaux.This includes the Kansas City Chiefs, the New Orleans Saints and the New England Patriots even though nothing has been confirmed yet.A few days ago, ESPN’s Jeremy Fowler and Dan Graziano predicted that the Kansas City Chiefs and the New Orleans Saints could be looking to trade for Kayvon Thibodeaux.In a recent report by ESPN, the NFL insiders noted, “In recent drafts, the Chiefs have been dumping picks into edge rusher to find more juice: 2023 first-rounder Felix Anudike-Uzomah, 2025 third-rounder Ashton Gillotte and 2026 second-rounder R Mason Thomas. Why not add one more dart throw in Thibodeaux, who can fill a designated pass-rush role while Gillotte takes more early-down work? The Chiefs do have an extra third-rounder next year as a result of the Trent McDuffie trade, but I don't think the Giants will be able to wrench a Day 2 pick from anyone for Thibodeaux, who has been only a rotational player to this point in his career.The report added, “The Giants would likely need a quality Day 2 pick to part with Thibodeaux, especially with Arvell Reese starting his career at inside linebacker. But New Orleans has inquired about Thibodeaux and still has a need at edge rusher after failing to reach a deal with Cameron Jordan thus far. In 2027, the Saints have a third-rounder and two fourth-rounders with which to maneuver.”For the New York Giants, trading Kayvon Thibodeaux would mean clearing his entire $14.75 million salary and facing no financial penalty for it.If the Kansas City Chiefs trade for Kayvon Thibodeaux, his $14.75 million contract might be a bit too expensive for the team. As per reports, the Chiefs might have to restructure a veteran’s contract for having enough room to fit Kayvon Thibodeaux on the roster.For the New Orleans Saints, it seems the team could sign a contract extension with Kayvon Thibodeaux. Instead of paying Kayvon Thibodeaux's full $14.75 million salary all at once in 2026, they might spread that cost out over several future years on paper, even though his contract ends after one season.For the New York Giants, while trading Kayvon Thibodeaux would mean losing a high potential edge rusher, it will also free up to $14.75 million which the team might use to fix other weak spots on the roster.If the Kansas City Chiefs sign Kayvon Thibodeaux, he would end up teaming with Chris Jones and George Karlaftis, which might prove pretty successful for the team.For the New Orleans Saints, Kayvon Thibodeaux would provide an immediate boost to an aging defense even though his contract might add more debt to the team.

Article Title: Who is Nolan Teasley? The Vikings' new GM, who had to beg 31 NFL teams for a chance
Article Text:
Image - X

Nolan Teasley went from begging NFL teams for a chance to becoming the Vikings’ new GM



What he walks into



The part that stays with you



Some people are built for the NFL. They play at elite programmes, get drafted, collect rings, and walk into front offices like they were always meant to be there. Nolan Teasley is not that story. His is better.In 2013, after six years working a marketing job, Teasley did something that takes a particular kind of stubborn, embarrassing, beautiful faith in yourself. He sat down and wrote letters to all 32 NFL teams. Not emails. Letters. Asking for a chance. Any chance. Thirty-one teams said nothing. One, the Seattle Seahawks, under GM John Schneider, gave him an interview. Then gave him an internship. In the scouting department. At the bottom.This weekend, thirteen years later, the Minnesota Vikings named Nolan Teasley their new General Manager.Teasley spent the next 13 seasons with Seattle, working every level of the organization, from scouting intern to the top of the front office, becoming assistant GM in 2023. There was no shortcut. He was a pro personnel scout from 2014 to 2016, assistant director of pro personnel in 2017, then director of pro personnel for five years before the promotion to assistant GM.Each title earned. Each year, another proof that the one team that bothered to respond had not made a mistake. In the 13 years Teasley was in Seattle, the Seahawks made the playoffs nine times and won two Super Bowls, including one just months ago. He was not a spectator in that success. He was credited with key personnel decisions that powered the Seahawks' most recent championship run, including drafts that delivered wide receiver Jaxon Smith-Njigba and safety Nick Emmanwori. The intern became indispensable.The Vikings fired Kwesi Adofo-Mensah earlier this year after a disappointing 9-8 season, less than a year after giving him a contract extension. Teasley now inherits a roster that has been through a turbulent rebuild, with Kyler Murray signed at quarterback and J.J. McCarthy still in the picture. It is not a tidy situation. It never is when a GM is fired mid-cycle.Teasley's hire comes with just three weeks left in the offseason programme and a mandatory minicamp beginning June 9. He has no runway. He hits the ground running, or he doesn't run at all. Given his history, the smart money is on the former.Thirty-one teams looked at Nolan Teasley's letter and decided he wasn't worth a conversation. One didn't. And that one decision, a single interview, a single internship offer, set in motion thirteen years of work that ends with him running an NFL franchise.Sport is full of stories about athletes who were told no and proved everyone wrong. We don't tell that story enough about the people in the building who never played a down. The scouts, the analysts, the interns who show up early and leave late and believe, without much evidence, that the work will eventually mean something.For Nolan Teasley, it meant everything. He just needed one team to open the door.
Article Summary: Nolan Teasley has been named as the Minnesota Vikings new General Manager. Teasley spent 13 years with the Seattle Seahawks, working every level of the organization, from scouting intern to the top of the front office. He was credited with key personnel decisions that powered the Seahawks' most recent championship run.

Article Title: Shedeur Sanders just broke Tom Brady’s record without barely playing in the NFL
Article Text:
(Credit: Getty Images)

Shedeur Sanders made 18x his NFL salary from licensing. Brady never came close



The number that puts it all in perspective



Shedeur Sanders slid to the fifth round of the 2025 NFL Draft. Teams passed on him 143 times. At the time, it looked like a financial disaster for one of college football's most marketable players. Turns out, the joke was on every team that passed. Sanders just broke the all-time NFL licensing record as a rookie who barely played.The NFLPA's annual federal filing shows Sanders received more than $17.7 million in royalties and player marketing income between May 2025 and February 2026, through his LLC SS2 Legendary, in thirteen separate payments, the largest being $9.24 million in a single payment on May 16, 2025.The previous all-time record belonged to Tom Brady, $9.5 million during the 2021-22 season. Sanders nearly doubled it. For further context, J.J. McCarthy led all NFL players in salary the season before at $4 million. Sanders made more than four times that. As a fifth-round pick. Sitting behind Joe Flacco on the depth chart. Group licensing income covers jersey sales, trading cards, video games, and collectibles, and none of this includes Sanders' personal brand deals with Gatorade, Delta Airlines, Beats by Dre, and Ralph Lauren.Factor those in, and he likely cleared well over $20 million off the field last season alone.Sanders' actual NFL salary last season was approximately $955,000. His licensing income was $17.7 million. He made roughly eighteen times more from fans buying his jersey and trading cards than from the Browns for playing football. That is not a typo.The licensing figure is also 170% more than what his father, Deion Sanders, will earn coaching Colorado in 2026. Prime Time built one of the most recognizable brands in sports history. His son just out-earned him without a starting job.Sanders didn't do this alone. His former University of Colorado teammate, Travis Hunter, came in second on the NFLPA list at $12.8 million, meaning two Buffaloes rookies finished first and second ahead of Patrick Mahomes , who earned $8 million in third. Colorado went from a rebuilding programme under Deion Sanders to producing the two most commercially powerful rookies in NFL history in a single draft class. Whatever you think of the hype, the market has spoken clearly.The NFL draft is supposed to determine a player's value. Shedeur Sanders' draft day was a humiliation in real time. Seven months later, he had already made more money than any player in league history from licensing before throwing a single meaningful pass. The fans who stuck with him through the slide made sure of that.
Article Summary: Shedeur Sanders made 18x his NFL salary from licensing. Previous all-time record belonged to Tom Brady, $9.5 million during the 2021-22 season. Sanders received more than $17.7 million in royalties and player marketing income between May 2025 and February 2026.

Article Title: Crissy Froyd exposed the NFL’s dirtiest secret after getting fired for speaking out
Article Text:
(Image via Getty)

Crissy Froyd risked everything, accusing NFL insiders of trading intimacy for access and now the league is silent

"attempting to blow the lid off the NFL's open secret."

The real question nobody wants to ask



Crissy Froyd was not a perfect messenger. She went scorched earth on X after losing her job. She made unverified claims. She had her own messy personal history tied to an NFL player. By every measure of "how to blow a whistle professionally," she got it wrong. And yet, the story she told deserves to be heard. Because nobody else was going to tell it.Froyd, a 26-year-old NFL reporter who had worked with USA Today since 2015, was fired on April 16 after publicly attacking Dianna Russini on X. Russini had just resigned from The Athletic following photos published by the New York Post showing her holding hands, hugging, and sitting poolside at a luxury Arizona resort with New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel, both of them married to other people. Froyd called it out. She used sharp words. Two days later, she was out of a job.In a Daily Mail op-ed published this week, Froyd escalated her claims from personal grievance to structural allegation. She says at least six female reporters told her first-hand they had slept with NFL coaches or staff from the teams they were assigned to cover, trading intimacy for access and stories. She says she was told about the Vrabel-Russini relationship more than five years ago. And she said the quiet part out loud: that she was fired, in part, forNone of this has been independently verified. Froyd's own credibility is complicated; she's a fired contractor, publicly angry, making enemies on social media.Froyd may believe every word she said, but that doesn't make every word publishable as fact.But the silence around it says something, tooHere is what is also true. Critics have long pointed out the inherent conflict of interest when "insider" journalists develop high-level social relationships with the very sources they are tasked with covering objectively.The NFL is not just a sport, it is a multi-billion-dollar media ecosystem where access is currency. Reporters who cover the league full-time need coaches, GMs, and team staff to pick up the phone. That dependency creates pressure. And pressure, over time, creates compromises.Russini was one of the highest-paid reporters. She had a podcast, a massive social following, and had traveled to Cannes with Times journalists to help woo advertisers. She was, in short, exactly the kind of journalist whose access to the NFL was worth money, real money to a media company. When the photos came out, the Athletic investigated the ethical concerns. Vrabel missed the final day of the NFL Draft to attend counseling and spend time with his family. Russini resigned. The NFL moved on. Vrabel kept coaching.Nobody lost their job except the woman who talked.Froyd's most pointed line in the Daily Mail piece may be her most important one: "Even more disheartening, it appears that no one is particularly interested in getting to the bottom of this story." She's right.The Athletic, acquired by The New York Times for $550 million in 2022, had positioned itself as the gold standard for sports journalism. If the allegation that reporter-source intimacy is a systemic feature of NFL coverage, not a one-off, were investigated seriously, it would implicate not just one reporter but an entire ecosystem of access journalism that major outlets have built their business models on. That is not a story anyone with skin in the game is eager to pursue. Froyd may have gone about this badly. But the question she raised- who is writing these stories, and what did they give to get them, is a legitimate one. In sports journalism, as in any beat where access is everything, the line between source relationship and conflict of interest has always been uncomfortably thin. The difference now is that someone finally said so. And she paid for it immediately.
Article Summary: Crissy Froyd, a 26-year-old NFL reporter who had worked with USA Today since 2015, was fired on April 16 after publicly attacking Dianna Russini on X. Russini had just resigned from The Athletic following photos published by the New York Post showing her holding hands, hugging, and sitting poolside at a luxury Arizona resort with New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel.

The Indianapolis Colts’ star player Anthony Richardson is actively seeking a trade since the last few weeks even as he continues to attend the team’s OTAs. While a number of teams have been linked to him, it seems the Green Bay Packers and the Detroit Lions have emerged as strong landing spots for the star player.While a number of rival teams have been linked to him, as per reports, the Green Bay Packers, Detroit Lions and the Minnesota Vikings have their eye on Anthony Richardson even though nothing has been confirmed yet.A few days ago, ESPN’s Jeremy Fowler and Dan Graziano predicted that the Green Bay Packers and the Detroit Lions could be looking to trade for Anthony Richardson.ESPN’s Jeremy Fowler and Dan Graziano noted in a recent report, “Richardson wants to be in a variation of the McVay-Shanahan-LaFleur system and took notice of what Packers coach Matt LaFleur did with Malik Willis. Green Bay signed Tyrod Taylor earlier this month but at a modest $2.5 million. With this trade, Indianapolis adds much-needed draft capital and moves forward with Riley Leonard as its No. 2 quarterback. And Green Bay asks Indy to cover a portion of Richardson's $5.4 million in 2026 guarantees. ”ESPN’s Jeremy Fowler and Dan Graziano added, “Backup quarterback has been an issue for the Lions, as Hendon Hooker and Kyle Allen have not been enough to stop the team from yanking Teddy Bridgewater away from high school coaching in Florida.General manager Brad Holmes and coach Dan Campbell tend to like quarterbacks with bigger bodies, so Richardson is a good fit. Campbell is an elite motivator who might help light a new fire under Richardson, either as a backup who prepares the right way or as a spot starter in the event of a Jared Goff injury. ”Anthony Richardson’s contract has presented a relatively unique situation for the star player.If he is traded before 1st August, an acquiring team would have to take on his entire $5.385 million guaranteed payout, including a large training camp roster bonus.But if he gets traded after 1st August, the Indianapolis Colts would have to pay the bonus, which will lower Richardson's incoming salary cap hit to $1.145 million which will be pretty affordable for his new team.For the Indianapolis Colts, trading Anthony Richardson would mean incurring a $5.43 million dead cap hit but it will also clear the path for rookie Riley Leonard to develop as the primary backup without any quarterback drama.For the Detroit Lions, adding Anthony Richardson would serve as an impactful backup behind Jared Goff.For the Green Bay Packers, trading for Anthony Richardson would be relatively affordable as someone who can step in without ruining the team's playoff hopes if Jordan Love gets hurt in the middle of the season.
Article Summary: The Indianapolis Colts’ star player Anthony Richardson is actively seeking a trade since the last few weeks. While a number of teams have been linked to him, it seems the Green Bay Packers and the Detroit Lions have emerged as strong landing spots for the star player.
